concerns.text: Tasks T031, T032, and T033 mandate implementing 'measurement uncertainty estimation' and 'calibration procedures' for fingerprint algorithms. This directly violates the spec's 'Assumptions' section which explicitly states: 'measurement uncertainty... is not recalculated' and 'no external calibration procedure is required'. The tasks are building features the spec explicitly forbids as unnecessary, constituting a semantic weakening of the project scope and a violation of the spec's defined constraints.

Task T028 implements mapping Morgan bits to phosphorus substructures but omits the specific success criterion threshold defined in SC-003. SC-003 requires: 'The sum of Gini importance for Morgan bits... must exceed the sum for MACCS keys by ≥ 15%'. The task description only mentions calculating the 'Gini importance sum' without mandating the verification of the ≥ 15% threshold, effectively dropping a critical acceptance criterion.

Constitution Principle VII and the Spec's Edge Cases require reporting 'Precision-Recall AUC' alongside ROC-AUC and Balanced Accuracy. Task T025 lists 'ROC-AUC, Precision-Recall AUC, and Balanced Accuracy', but Task T026 (t-test) and T027 (bootstrap) do not explicitly mandate performing these statistical validations on the PR-AUC metric, only implying it via the general 'evaluate' scope. Given the strict statistical rigor required, the tasks should explicitly state that the t-test and bootstrapping apply to the PR-AUC difference as well to ensure the SC-001/Constitution VII requirement is not silently relaxed.
